c74object.name: voice-alligator~
c74object.digest: Signal-domain voice allocator with built-in ADSR, glide, and velocity scaling
c74object.description:
voice-alligator~ is the signal-domain companion to voice-alligator. It performs the same voice allocation and shares the same inlet 1 message protocol, but replaces the message outlet with nine MC signal outlets which output the volume envelope and pitch plus the flags. The ADSR envelope, glide interpolation, and velocity scaling are computed internally — no adsr~, line~, or feedback inlet is needed in the voice patcher.
For full documentation of the shared voice allocation behaviour — including streams, hold, sustain, mono, steal, scale definitions, and the inlet 1 message format — see the voice-alligator reference.
Features added over voice-alligator:
- Built-in ADSR
- Built-in glide / portamento with linear or logarithmic curvature.
- All signal outlets are MC — one channel per voice.
c74object.discussion:
Inlet 1 — Note input
Identical to voice-alligator inlet 1. See that reference for the full message syntax.
MC Signal Outlets
Outlet 1 — freq~
Frequency in Hz, glide-interpolated. Use this to drive your oscillator pitch directly. Interpolation is controlled by glidetime and glidetime_curvature.
Outlet 2 — env~
ADSR envelope output, velocity-scaled. The peak of the envelope equals the normalised velocity (0–1), so this signal can be used directly as an amplitude multiplier. ADSR shape is controlled by the attack, decay, sustain_level, release, and curve attributes.
Outlet 3 — impulse~
A single sample impulse fired at the start of each note attack. Use this to trigger pitch envelopes or other one-shot modulations in the voice. glide_impulse controls whether an impulse is fired on glide notes.
Outlet 4 — glide flag~
1.0 while a glide (portamento) is in progress, 0.0 otherwise.
Outlet 5 — hold flag~
1.0 while the voice is in hold, 0.0 otherwise. Gets set on note-off.
Outlet 6 — sustain flag~
1.0 while the voice is in sustain, 0.0 otherwise. Gets set on note-off.
Outlet 7 — sequencer flag~
1.0 if the current note is a sequencer note, 0.0 otherwise.
Outlet 8 — mono flag~
1.0 if the current note is a mono note, 0.0 otherwise. Gets set on note-on.
Outlet 9 — stream flag~
stream number of the current note. Gets set on note-on.
Outlet 10 — message outlet
Outputs the same target / notes / flags messages as voice-alligator outlet 1. Primarily for usage with voice-alligator-looper.
ADSR parameter changes
ADSR parameter changes (attack, decay, sustain_level, release, curves) take effect on the next note-on only. Currently playing voices are not affected.

objarg.name: voices
objarg.description:
Number of voices. Maximum: 1024. Default: 1.
Unlike voice-alligator, the number of voices cannot be changed after construction — the voices message is not supported. Set this argument to match the first argument of your mc.poly~.

method.name: list
method.description:
Identical to voice-alligator inlet 1. Full syntax:
midipitch, velocity, (stream), (mono_flag), (realpitch)
See the voice-alligator reference for full documentation of all arguments.

method.name: end
method.description:
end sends all notes into release.
end $1 sends notes of stream $1 into release.

method.name: endhold
method.description: Releases all hold notes when called with endhold, ends the youngest held note with endhold first, or the oldest with endhold last.

method.name: scale_def
method.description: Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference for full documentation.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print active voices and all parameter settings to console
method.description: Prints active voices, inactive voices, and the current value of every parameter to the Max console. Useful for debugging.

attribute.name: active
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: true

attribute.name: mono
attribute.description:
Enables monophonic / portamento playing. Identical behaviour to voice-alligator.
Glide notes output 1.0 on outlet 3 (glide~) and do not fire an impulse on outlet 8. Whether the envelope is retriggered on a glide note is controlled by glide_retrigger.
default: false

attribute.name: mono_note_priority
attribute.digest: Mono note priority: 0 last, 1 low, 2 high
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: 0 (Last)

attribute.name: mono_steals_release
attribute.digest: Mono note steals releasing mono notes
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: false

attribute.name: hold
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. While a voice is in hold, outlet 4 (hold~) outputs 1.0.
default: false

attribute.name: sustain
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. While a voice is in sustain, outlet 5 (sustain~) outputs 1.0.
default: false

attribute.name: steal
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: true

attribute.name: steal_hold
attribute.digest: Allow hold notes to be stolen (default true)
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: true

attribute.name: respect_stream_priorities
attribute.digest: Stream-aware stealing: 0 off, 1 strict, 2 prefer lower priority
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: 1

attribute.name: output_mode
attribute.description:
Determines whether outlet 1 (freq~) outputs frequencies in Hz or raw MIDI pitch values.
default: 1 (Frequency)

attribute.name: scale_def_mode
attribute.digest: Scale definition mode: 0 MIDI, 1 frequency (default frequency)
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator. See that reference.
default: 1 (Frequency)

attribute.name: basefreq
attribute.description:
Identical to voice-alligator.
default: 440.0

attribute.name: debug
attribute.description:
Prints note-on, note-off, voice assignment, and ADSR completion events to the Max console.
default: false

attribute.name: attack
attribute.description:
Attack time of the built-in ADSR in milliseconds. Takes effect on the next note-on; running voices are unaffected.
default: 10 ms

attribute.name: decay
attribute.description:
Decay time of the built-in ADSR in milliseconds.
default: 100 ms

attribute.name: sustain_level
attribute.description:
Sustain level of the built-in ADSR as a fraction of peak (0–1).
If sustain_level is set to 0.0, we have an ADS envelope with no release phase. We don't need to provide note-off messages to end the envelope, and the hold and sustain attributes have limited effect.
default: 1.0

attribute.name: release
attribute.description:
Release time of the built-in ADSR in milliseconds.
default: 300 ms

attribute.name: attack_curve
attribute.digest: Attack curve shape: negative concave, 0 linear, positive convex (default 0)
attribute.description:
Shape of the attack segment. Negative values produce a concave (slow start) curve, positive values a convex (fast start) curve, 0 is linear.
default: 0.0

attribute.name: decay_curve
attribute.description:
Shape of the decay segment. Same range and convention as attack_curve.
default: 0

attribute.name: release_curve
attribute.description:
Shape of the release segment. Same range and convention as attack_curve.
default: 0.0

attribute.name: declick_ms
attribute.digest: Declick ramp time in ms for retriggered voices (default 5)
attribute.description:
When a voice is retriggered while its ADSR is still active, the envelope ramps to zero over this duration before the new attack begins. This prevents clicks when notes overlap. Impulses out of outlet 8 are fired at the bottom of this declick ramp.
default: 5 ms

attribute.name: glidetime
attribute.description:
Time in milliseconds for outlet 1 (freq~) to glide from the previous pitch to the new pitch. 0 = instant snap.
default: 30 ms

attribute.name: glidetime_curvature
attribute.digest: Glide interpolation: 0 linear Hz, 1 logarithmic semitones (default 0)
attribute.description:
Blends between linear Hz interpolation (0) and logarithmic / semitone interpolation (1). Values between 0 and 1 are a proportional blend. Log interpolation sounds more natural for large pitch intervals.
default: 0.0 (linear)

attribute.name: glide_retrigger
attribute.digest: Retrigger envelope on glide notes (default false)
attribute.description:
Controls envelope behaviour on glide (portamento) notes.
0 (legato): The envelope runs completely uninterrupted through the glide.
1 (retrigger): The ADSR keeps running, and a gain ramp from the current velocity ratio to the new velocity is applied over the attack time. This produces a velocity change without a declick.
default: false

attribute.name: glide_impulse
attribute.digest: Send impulse on outlet 8 when a glide note is triggered (default false)
attribute.description:
Controls whether an impulse is sent on outlet 8 when a glide note is triggered.
default: false
